name: ARK: Survival Evolved Dedicated Server
description: ARK: Survival Evolved dedicated server, installed via SteamCMD (anonymous). Large download (~15 GB). Supports workshop mods (-automanagedmods) and cross-server clusters.

configFiles.description: GameUserSettings.ini and Game.ini, created after first run
World save data, created after first run

variables.label: Workshop mod ids (comma-separated, blank = no mods)
variables.description: Steam Workshop mod ids in load order, e.g. 731604991,889745138. Written to GameUserSettings.ini on install/update; the server downloads and installs them itself at startup (-automanagedmods), which can take a while on first start.

variables.label: Cluster id (blank = no cluster)
variables.description: Set the SAME id on every ARK instance that should allow cross-server transfers (obelisk/terminal uploads). Pick something non-obvious. All members share this host's cluster directory automatically.

notes: First start takes several minutes with little console output while ARK loads - be patient and watch CPU usage. Mods: list workshop ids in the mod field, then click Update files (writes ActiveMods to GameUserSettings.ini); the server downloads mods itself at startup, which adds significant time on first run. Cluster: create two or more ARK instances (e.g. different maps), set the same Cluster id on each, and players can transfer via obelisks/terminals; servers in a cluster must not run the same map save. The peer port (game port + 1) must also be open. ARK is stopped with SIGINT (Unreal's graceful shutdown) - stopping can take up to a minute while the world saves. Requires a high open-files limit (LimitNOFILE=100000 in the systemd unit - re-run scripts/install.sh once after updating if your unit predates this).
